test_cases.test_case: Successful login redirects teacher to Dashboard
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: redirects teacher to Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Access as a guest enables unauthenticated browsing
steps: 1. Click the Access as a guest button
test_cases.expected_result: grants unauthenticated browsing

test_cases.test_case: Cookies notice displays cookie usage information
steps: 1. Click the Cookies notice button
test_cases.expected_result: shows cookie usage information

test_cases.test_case: Lost password link is present but disabled on this test site
steps: 1. Inspect the 'Lost password?' link on the page
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Lost password?' link is visible but disabled on this test site

test_cases.test_case: Username (text) required: leave Username blank and submit
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Username field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; the user remains on the Login page; no redirect to Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Password (password) required: leave Password blank and submit
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Password field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; the user remains on the Login page; Password field remains empty

test_cases.test_case: All required fields blank: submit with Username and Password empty
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on both Username and Password fields indicating they are required; the form does not submit; the user remains on the Login page; no redirect to Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Invalid credentials: submit with incorrect password
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <registered username> in the Username field
2. Enter <incorrect password> in the Password field
3. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error message is shown indicating credentials are invalid; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the entered value; the user remains on the Login page and is not redirected to Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Precondition: 'Lost password?' link disabled on test site
preconditions: User is on the Login page
Test site configuration where Lost password? is disabled
steps: 1. Click the Lost password? link
test_cases.expected_result: The Lost password? link is disabled/unavailable and is not actionable; clicking it does not navigate to a password recovery page; no password recovery flow is started

test_cases.test_case: Very long Username is retained after failed login
steps: 1. Enter <very long string (200+ characters)> into the Username field
2. Enter an incorrect value into the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Login attempt is blocked; an inline error message is shown (is blocked / error shown); the Password field is cleared (visible empty). The Username field remains populated with the full <very long string> as displayed in the UI (retention of the entered Username succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Username and Password containing emoji/special Unicode are handled on failed login
steps: 1. Enter a Username containing emoji and non-ASCII characters into the Username field
2. Enter a Password containing emoji and non-ASCII characters into the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Login attempt is blocked; an inline error message is shown (is blocked / error shown); the Password field is cleared (visible empty). The Username field retains and displays the exact entered characters (including emoji/unicode) as shown in the UI (retention succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in Username is preserved/retained after failed login
steps: 1. Enter a Username into the Username field that includes leading and trailing whitespace (e.g., starts and ends with space characters)
2. Enter an incorrect value into the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: Login attempt is blocked; an inline error message is shown (is blocked / error shown); the Password field is cleared (visible empty). The Username field continues to display the entered value as shown in the UI (including the leading/trailing whitespace) — the retention of the entered Username succeeds (visible exact value retained).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive failed submissions: password cleared and username retained across attempts
steps: 1. Enter a Username into the Username field
2. Enter an incorrect Password into the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
4. Immediately (without changing the Username) enter a different incorrect Password into the Password field
5. Click the 'Log in' button again
test_cases.expected_result: Each login attempt is blocked; an inline error message is shown after each attempt (is blocked / error shown). After every failed attempt the Password field is cleared (visible empty) and the Username field remains populated with the original entered Username (retention succeeds across rapid consecutive failures).

test_cases.test_case: Timeline: search filters activities by name or type
preconditions: Timeline contains multiple activities including at least one activity matching <search term> and at least one unrelated activity
steps: 1. Enter <search term> in the Search_Activities field in the Timeline block
2. Press Enter or click the Search icon
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline displays only activities matching <search term> by name or type; unrelated activities are no longer visible in the Timeline list

test_cases.test_case: Timeline: empty state shown when no items exist in selected time range
preconditions: No timeline items exist within the <Next 7 days> time range
steps: 1. Select 'Next 7 days' from the Time_Range dropdown in the Timeline block
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline block shows the empty-state UI indicating there are no items within the selected time range

test_cases.test_case: Timeline: changing sort order reorders the activity list
preconditions: Timeline contains at least two activities with differing date/time or priority values
steps: 1. Select <Sort option> from the Sort_Order dropdown in the Timeline block
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline lists activities in the order defined by the selected <Sort option>; the visible order of activities updates to reflect that sort criteria

test_cases.test_case: Calendar: course filter shows only events for selected course
preconditions: Calendar month contains events from multiple courses including <Course A> and <Course B>
steps: 1. Select <Course A> from the Course_Filter dropdown in the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar month grid displays only events belonging to <Course A>; events from other courses are no longer visible in the calendar

test_cases.test_case: Calendar: create a new event using New Event button
preconditions: Calendar is showing the target month and date where the event will be created
steps: 1. Click the New_Event button in the Calendar block
2. In the New Event modal, enter <valid event title>, select <date> and fill required details
3. Click Save (or Create) in the New Event modal
test_cases.expected_result: A new event is visible on the selected date in the calendar grid and that date displays the event name inline

test_cases.test_case: Calendar: navigate to next month using Next arrow
preconditions: Calendar is showing the current month
steps: 1. Click the Next_Month button (right arrow) in the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar heading updates to show the next month and year in the Calendar block

test_cases.test_case: Calendar: navigate to previous month using Previous arrow
preconditions: Calendar is showing the current month
steps: 1. Click the Previous_Month button (left arrow) in the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar heading updates to show the previous month and year in the Calendar block

test_cases.test_case: Calendar: current date is highlighted in the month view
preconditions: Calendar is showing the month that contains the current date
steps: 1. Click the Calendar block to focus it
test_cases.expected_result: The cell corresponding to the current date is visually highlighted in the calendar grid

test_cases.test_case: Calendar: dates with events display event names inline
preconditions: Calendar contains at least one event on <date> within the visible month
steps: 1. Click the Calendar block to focus it
test_cases.expected_result: The calendar cell for <date> shows the event name inline within the month grid

test_cases.test_case: Calendar: Full calendar link opens the dedicated calendar view
steps: 1. Click the Full_Calendar link at the bottom of the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: The dedicated calendar view is opened (user is navigated to the calendar page/view)

test_cases.test_case: Calendar: Import or Export Calendars link opens calendar data management
steps: 1. Click the Import_or_Export_Calendars link at the bottom of the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: The calendar data management view is opened (import/export calendar settings or UI is displayed)

test_cases.test_case: New event button does not create calendar entry
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
Calendar is visible in the Calendar block
steps: 1. Click the 'New event' button in the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: No new calendar entry is created; the calendar view still shows the same events and dates (no added event on <date_of_attempt>); there is no success confirmation; an error notification or validation message is displayed and the Dashboard remains unchanged (the 'creates calendar entry' on_success did not occur)

test_cases.test_case: Full calendar link fails to open dedicated calendar view
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard and the Calendar block is visible
steps: 1. Click the 'Full calendar' link at the bottom of the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: The dedicated calendar view does not open; the user remains on the Dashboard (URL does not navigate to the full calendar view); no calendar page content is displayed and an inline error notification or no action is shown (the 'opens dedicated calendar view' on_success did not occur)

test_cases.test_case: Import or export calendars link does not open calendar data management
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard and the Calendar block is visible
steps: 1. Click the 'Import or export calendars' link at the bottom of the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: The calendar data management view does not open; the user remains on the Dashboard (no navigation or modal for import/export appears); an inline error notification or no action is shown (the 'opens calendar data management' on_success did not occur)

test_cases.test_case: Dates with events do not display event names inline
preconditions: An event exists on <date_with_event> in the calendar for the currently selected month
User is on the Dashboard and viewing the month containing <date_with_event>
steps: 1. Observe the calendar cell for <date_with_event> in the monthly view
test_cases.expected_result: The calendar cell for <date_with_event> does not display the event name inline; there is no inline text or event label shown on that date despite an event existing (violates 'dates with events display their event names inline'); the event is not visible on the monthly view and no inline indicator is present

test_cases.test_case: Current date is not highlighted in the calendar
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard and viewing the month that contains <current_date>
steps: 1. Open the Dashboard (or navigate to the Calendar block for the current month)
test_cases.expected_result: The cell for <current_date> is not visually highlighted (no highlight, border, or distinct styling indicating the current date); the current month and year heading may still be correct but the 'Current_Date_Highlight' visible_when condition is not fulfilled visually

test_cases.test_case: Event on exactly the last day of the selected 'Next 7 days' range appears in Timeline and Calendar
preconditions: User is logged in and Dashboard is open
steps: 1. Click the Time_Range dropdown in the Timeline_Block
2. Select 'Next 7 days' from the Time_Range dropdown
3. Click the Calendar_Block New_Event button
4. In the New Event form, set the event date to the date exactly 7 days from today (the last day of the selected range)
5. Fill any other required fields in the New Event form
6. Click Save/Create to create the event
7. Observe the Timeline_Block activity list and the date cell for the event in the Calendar_Block
test_cases.expected_result: Event creation succeeds; the newly created event is listed in the Timeline_Block (visible in the activity list) and the Calendar_Block date cell for the date exactly 7 days from today displays the event name inline (Form presence in Timeline and inline display succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Event scheduled one day after the end of the 'Next 7 days' range is excluded from Timeline
preconditions: User is logged in and Dashboard is open
Timeline_Block Time_Range dropdown is visible and set to 'Next 7 days'
steps: 1. Ensure Time_Range in the Timeline_Block is set to 'Next 7 days'
2. Click the Calendar_Block New_Event button
3. In the New Event form, set the event date to the date that is exactly 8 days from today (one day after the selected range)
4. Fill any other required fields in the New Event form
5. Click Save/Create to create the event
6. Observe the Timeline_Block activity list
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline_Block does not list the event created for the date one day after the selected range; the Timeline_Block shows its Empty_State or absence of that event (is blocked / error shown for inclusion in the selected range).

test_cases.test_case: Very long search string (200+ characters) in Search_Activities field
preconditions: User is logged in and Dashboard is open
Search_Activities field in Timeline_Block is visible
steps: 1. Click into the Search_Activities field in the Timeline_Block
2. Enter a very long string of 200+ characters into the Search_Activities field
3. Press Enter or click the Search control to execute the search
4. Observe the Timeline_Block results area
test_cases.expected_result: Search executes successfully without UI crash; the Timeline_Block displays either matching activities or a 'no results' message and the search input remains usable (long input handling succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Search with special characters and emoji in Search_Activities
preconditions: User is logged in and Dashboard is open
Search_Activities field in Timeline_Block is visible
steps: 1. Click into the Search_Activities field in the Timeline_Block
2. Enter a string containing special characters and emoji (e.g., punctuation, symbols, emoji) into the Search_Activities field
3. Press Enter or click the Search control to execute the search
4. Observe the Timeline_Block results area and any inline validation or error messages
test_cases.expected_result: Search executes successfully with special characters and emoji; no validation error is shown and the results area either lists matching activities or shows 'no results' (special-character search succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive Next_Month clicks advances calendar by multiple months without UI failure and current-date highlight updates accordingly
preconditions: User is logged in and Dashboard is open
Calendar_Block is visible with Current month/year heading and Next_Month button
steps: 1. Note the current month/year heading in the Calendar_Block
2. Click the Next_Month button five times in rapid succession (fast clicks, less than one second between clicks)
3. Observe the Calendar_Block month/year heading after the rapid clicks
4. Check whether Current_Date_Highlight is visible in the displayed month
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar_Block updates to the month that is five months ahead and the UI remains responsive; if the displayed month no longer contains today's date, the Current_Date_Highlight is not visible; no duplicate navigation actions or UI errors occur (rapid navigation succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Current date highlight toggles when navigating away from and back to the current month
preconditions: User is logged in and Dashboard is open
Calendar_Block is visible and Current_Date_Highlight is visible for the current month
steps: 1. Click the Calendar_Block Previous_Month button once
2. Observe that Current_Date_Highlight is not visible in the previous month view
3. Click the Calendar_Block Next_Month button once to return to the original month
4. Observe that Current_Date_Highlight is visible again in the current month view
test_cases.expected_result: When viewing the previous month Current_Date_Highlight is not shown; after returning to the current month the Current_Date_Highlight is visible again (highlight toggle succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Enable Edit mode shows edit controls
steps: 1. Check the Edit mode checkbox
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Reset page to default' button is visible in the top-right, the '+ Add a block' button is visible below the Dashboard heading, and each dashboard block shows a move handle and an options menu.

test_cases.test_case: Disable Edit mode hides edit controls
steps: 1. Check the Edit mode checkbox to enable Edit mode
2. Uncheck the Edit mode checkbox to disable Edit mode
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Reset page to default' button and the '+ Add a block' button are no longer visible, and move handles and options menus are not visible on dashboard blocks.

test_cases.test_case: Open Add a block page from + Add a block button
steps: 1. Click the '+ Add a block' button
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Add a block' page is displayed listing: Comments, Course overview, Latest announcements, Latest badges, Learning plans, Logged in user, Mentees, Online users, Private files, Random glossary entry, Recently accessed courses, Starred courses, Tags, Text, and Upcoming events.

test_cases.test_case: Add a 'Text' block via Add action
steps: 1. Click the '+ Add a block' button
2. On the Add a block page, click Add next to 'Text'
test_cases.expected_result: A 'Text' block appears on the Dashboard in the current layout.

test_cases.test_case: Cancel from Add a block returns to Dashboard without adding
steps: 1. Click the '+ Add a block' button
2. Click the Cancel link on the Add a block page
test_cases.expected_result: The Add a block page closes and the Dashboard is displayed with no new block added.

test_cases.test_case: Reset page to default reverts a customized layout
steps: 1. Click the 'Reset page to default' button
test_cases.expected_result: reverts layout to default for current user

test_cases.test_case: Reorder a block by dragging its move handle
preconditions: There are multiple blocks on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Drag the move handle of <target block> to a new position within the Dashboard
test_cases.expected_result: <target block> is displayed in the new position in the Dashboard.

test_cases.test_case: Open Configure from a block's options menu
preconditions: There is a <target block> on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click the options menu on <target block>
2. Click Configure in the options menu
test_cases.expected_result: The configuration UI for <target block> is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Delete a block via the options menu
preconditions: There is a <target block> on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click the options menu on <target block>
2. Click Delete in the options menu
3. Click Confirm on the Delete dialog
test_cases.expected_result: <target block> is no longer present on the Dashboard.

test_cases.test_case: Move a block using the options menu Move action
preconditions: There are multiple blocks on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click the options menu on <target block>
2. Click Move in the options menu
3. Use the Move UI to place <target block> in the desired new position and apply the change
test_cases.expected_result: <target block> is displayed in the selected new position in the Dashboard.

test_cases.test_case: Reset page to default is not available when Edit mode is off
steps: 1. Ensure Edit mode is off
2. Look at the top-right area of the Dashboard for a 'Reset page to default' button
3. Attempt to click a hidden or missing 'Reset page to default' control (if present)
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Reset page to default' button is not present/visible when Edit mode is off; it cannot be clicked; the Dashboard layout is not reverted and remains unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: '+ Add a block' button is not visible or clickable when Edit mode is off
steps: 1. Ensure Edit mode is off
2. Inspect below the Dashboard heading for the '+ Add a block' button
3. Attempt to click '+ Add a block' if it appears
test_cases.expected_result: The '+ Add a block' button is not visible when Edit mode is off; it cannot be clicked or used to navigate to the Add a block page; no Add a block page is opened and no block can be added.

test_cases.test_case: Adding a block is blocked if Edit mode is turned off after opening Add a block page
preconditions: User is viewing their Dashboard containing at least one block
steps: 1. Toggle Edit mode on
2. Click '+ Add a block' to open the Add a block page
3. On the Add a block page, toggle Edit mode off
4. Click the 'Add' action for any listed block row
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Add' action is disabled or has no effect when Edit mode is not on; no new block is added to the Dashboard; the Dashboard layout remains unchanged and the Add a block page does not add or persist any block.

test_cases.test_case: Move (drag) handle is not available and blocks cannot be dragged when Edit mode is off
preconditions: User is viewing their Dashboard containing at least one block
steps: 1. Ensure Edit mode is off
2. Attempt to locate the move handle (drag icon) on any existing block
3. Attempt to drag a block using the move handle
test_cases.expected_result: Move handle (drag icon) is not visible when Edit mode is off; dragging is not possible; no layout changes occur and the block remains in its original position.

test_cases.test_case: Options menu (three-dot) and its actions are not available when Edit mode is off
preconditions: User is viewing their Dashboard containing at least one block
steps: 1. Ensure Edit mode is off
2. Attempt to open the three-dot options menu for any block
3. If the menu is visible, attempt to select 'Configure', 'Move', and 'Delete'
test_cases.expected_result: The three-dot options menu is not visible when Edit mode is off; Configure/Move/Delete actions are not accessible; no block configuration, movement, or deletion occurs and the block remains unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: Block Delete action is blocked if Edit mode is turned off after opening the options menu
preconditions: User is viewing their Dashboard containing at least one block
steps: 1. Toggle Edit mode on
2. Open the options menu (three-dot) for a block and reveal the 'Delete' action
3. Toggle Edit mode off while the options menu is open
4. Click the 'Delete' action
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Delete' action is disabled or has no effect when Edit mode is not on; the block is not removed from the Dashboard; no layout change is persisted and the block remains present.

test_cases.test_case: Add action blocked when Edit mode is turned off while Add a block page is open
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Toggle Edit mode to on
2. Click the "+ Add a block" button
3. On the Add a block page, toggle Edit mode to off
4. On the Add a block page, click the Add action for any row (for example, the first row in the list)
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking Add is blocked / error shown: the Add action does not add a block to the Dashboard; the Add control is disabled or an inline message is shown indicating Edit mode must be on

test_cases.test_case: Adding the same block type twice in quick succession persists two instances
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
Block type exists in Add a block list (e.g., Text)
steps: 1. Toggle Edit mode to on
2. Click the "+ Add a block" button
3. On the Add a block page, click Add for the chosen block type
4. Wait for navigation back to the Dashboard (layout updated)
5. Click the "+ Add a block" button again
6. On the Add a block page, click Add for the same block type a second time
7. Refresh the Dashboard page
test_cases.expected_result: Both adds succeed: two instances of the chosen block type appear in the Dashboard_Blocks repeating group and the two instances persist after page refresh (layout changes are persisted per user)

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive drag moves: final position persists as last move
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
Dashboard has at least two blocks present in Dashboard_Blocks
steps: 1. Toggle Edit mode to on
2. Using the move handle, drag block A from its original position to a new position (e.g., move to top)
3. Immediately drag the same block A back to its previous/original position
4. Refresh the Dashboard page to force persistence reload
5. Inspect the order of blocks in the Dashboard_Blocks repeating group
test_cases.expected_result: The final drag move succeeds and is persisted: after refresh the order of blocks matches the last move performed (the last move's order is visible and retained)

test_cases.test_case: Reset page to default is not available or actionable when Edit mode is off
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Ensure Edit mode is toggled off
2. Attempt to locate and click the "Reset page to default" button in the top-right
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked / error shown: the "Reset page to default" button is not visible or is disabled when Edit mode == off and the reset action cannot be triggered via the visible UI

test_cases.test_case: Delete all blocks then use Reset page to default to restore default layout
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
Dashboard has at least one block
steps: 1. Toggle Edit mode to on
2. For each block in the Dashboard_Blocks repeating group, open the Options menu and click Delete (repeat until no blocks remain)
3. Observe the Dashboard when zero blocks remain
4. Click the "Reset page to default" button
5. Refresh the Dashboard page
test_cases.expected_result: Deleting the last block succeeds and the Dashboard shows an empty/zero-block state; clicking "Reset page to default" succeeds and the default dashboard layout is restored and persisted for the current user (after refresh default blocks are present)

test_cases.test_case: Configure action with very long free-text input in block configuration
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Dashboard
At least one block has an Options menu with a Configure action
steps: 1. Toggle Edit mode to on
2. Open the Options menu for a block that supports Configure
3. Click Configure to open the configuration UI
4. In a free-text configuration field, enter a very long string (greater than 200 characters)
5. Click Save in the configuration UI
6. Open the block's display or configuration again to view the stored value
test_cases.expected_result: Save succeeds: the saved configuration displays the entered long text (if the UI truncates the value, truncation is visibly indicated and the displayed text reflects the stored/truncated value); no unhandled UI error occurs

test_cases.test_case: Star a course from the card menu pins it to the top
preconditions: <target course> is visible in the Courses grid and is not currently starred
steps: 1. Click the Card Menu (three-dot) on <target course>
2. Click 'Star this course' in the menu
test_cases.expected_result: The selected course is pinned to the top of the Courses grid: the course card appears in the first position and displays a 'Starred' status/badge

test_cases.test_case: Remove a course from view via the card menu hides it from the grid
preconditions: <target course> is visible in the Courses grid
steps: 1. Click the Card Menu (three-dot) on <target course>
2. Click 'Remove from view' in the menu
test_cases.expected_result: The selected course is no longer visible in the Courses grid (it has been hidden from view)

test_cases.test_case: Click course name link navigates to the course main page
preconditions: <target course> is visible in the Courses grid
steps: 1. Click the Course Name link on <target course>
test_cases.expected_result: The application navigates to the Course main page for <target course> (redirect to the course main page is triggered)

test_cases.test_case: Status filter set to 'Starred' shows only starred courses
preconditions: At least one course is Starred and at least one course is not Starred in the grid
steps: 1. Open the Status filter dropdown
2. Select 'Starred' from the Status filter
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid displays only courses with the 'Starred' status; non-starred courses are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Search filters the courses grid by query
preconditions: Multiple courses with distinct names are visible in the Courses grid
steps: 1. Enter <search query> in the Search field
2. Press Enter or trigger search
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid displays only rows matching <search query>; unrelated courses are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Change layout to List updates the courses display
preconditions: Courses are currently displayed in the default layout
steps: 1. Open the Layout dropdown
2. Select 'List' from the Layout dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid switches to List layout: course items are displayed in list rows instead of cards

test_cases.test_case: Select a sort criterion updates the course ordering
preconditions: Multiple courses with orderable attributes are visible in the Courses grid
steps: 1. Open the Sort dropdown
2. Select <sort criterion> from the Sort dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid is reordered according to <sort criterion>; the visible order of course cards updates so the first card reflects the applied sort

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user attempts to access My Courses page
steps: 1. Ensure the user session is logged out
2. Navigate to the <My Courses page> (e.g., enter the My Courses URL or click the My Courses link)
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: the user is redirected to the login page; the Login form is displayed and the My Courses content (course cards, toolbar) is not shown. No course cards are visible.

test_cases.test_case: User with wrong role attempts to access My Courses (teacher-only) page
preconditions: User is authenticated as <non-teacher role> (e.g., Student)
steps: 1. Log in as a user with role <non-teacher role>
2. Navigate to the <My Courses page>
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked: the page does not display My Courses content. An authorization error or insufficient-permission indicator is shown (e.g., an access denied message) and no course cards or teacher-only controls are visible; the user is not able to view or interact with the My Courses page (user is either redirected away or remains on current page).

test_cases.test_case: Very long search query (200+ characters)
preconditions: User is signed in and on the My Courses page
At least one course is present in the Courses_Grid
steps: 1. Click the Search field in the toolbar
2. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) into the Search field
3. Press Enter or click the search icon
test_cases.expected_result: Search executes without an inline error; the Search field displays the long string and the Courses_Grid updates to show matching course cards (or a visible 'No results' indicator) — action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Search with special characters and emoji
preconditions: User is signed in and on the My Courses page
At least one course is present in the Courses_Grid
steps: 1. Click the Search field in the toolbar
2. Enter a search string containing special characters and emoji (e.g. punctuation, symbols, emoji)
3. Press Enter or click the search icon
test_cases.expected_result: Search executes without an inline error; the Search field displays the entered special characters/emoji and the Courses_Grid updates to show matching course cards (or a visible 'No results' indicator) — action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Star a course from deep in the list pins it to the top
preconditions: User is signed in and on the My Courses page
There are multiple courses such that target course is not in a first-row visible position
steps: 1. Scroll the Courses_Grid so the target course card is visible (not already at the top)
2. Click the card's Card_Menu (three-dot menu)
3. Click the 'Star this course' action
test_cases.expected_result: The course's status is set to Starred and its card is moved to the top of the Courses_Grid (pinned position) and no error is shown — action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Repeat 'Star this course' on an already Starred course (idempotency)
preconditions: User is signed in and on the My Courses page
A course is already in Starred status and appears at the top
steps: 1. Locate the already Starred course at the top of the Courses_Grid
2. Open that card's Card_Menu
3. Click the 'Star this course' action again
test_cases.expected_result: No duplicate card is created; only one card for the course remains at the top and no error is shown. The repeated action is effectively a no-op (status remains Starred) — action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Remove from view (hide) a course then verify it appears under Hidden filter
preconditions: User is signed in and on the My Courses page
At least one course is present in the Courses_Grid
steps: 1. Identify a visible course card in the Courses_Grid
2. Open that card's Card_Menu
3. Click the 'Remove from view' action
4. Observe the Courses_Grid after the action
5. Open the Status_Filter dropdown and select Hidden
test_cases.expected_result: After step 3 the course card is removed from the current (All) view with no error shown. After selecting Status_Filter = Hidden the previously hidden course appears in the Courses_Grid (status is Hidden) — both actions succeed

test_cases.test_case: Star a course while viewing Hidden filter — status transition from Hidden to Starred
preconditions: User is signed in and on the My Courses page
There is at least one course currently with status Hidden
steps: 1. Locate a Hidden course card in the Courses_Grid
2. Open that card's Card_Menu
3. Click the 'Star this course' action
4. Observe the Courses_Grid while still on Status_Filter = Hidden
5. Change Status_Filter to All and observe the Courses_Grid
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking 'Star this course' sets the course status to Starred. Immediately after step 3 the course is removed from the Hidden filter view (no error shown). After switching Status_Filter to All the course appears at the top of the list as Starred — the starring transition succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Toggle a single section collapsed state
preconditions: Course Page is open and displays at least one expanded section <section>
steps: 1. Click the 'Toggle Section Collapse' button (chevron) for <section>
test_cases.expected_result: toggles this section collapsed state

test_cases.test_case: Collapse all sections using the top‑right link
preconditions: Course Page is open with multiple expanded sections
steps: 1. Click the 'Collapse all' link in the top-right of the sections list
test_cases.expected_result: collapses all sections

test_cases.test_case: Open an activity or resource from a section
preconditions: Course Page is open with at least one expanded section containing an activity <activity>
steps: 1. Click the activity name link 'Open activity/resource' for <activity> inside the section
test_cases.expected_result: opens activity or resource

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to a tab via the navigation tab bar
preconditions: Course Page is open showing the navigation tab bar
steps: 1. Click the <tab name> tab in the navigation tab bar
2. Observe the main content area for the selected tab's panel
test_cases.expected_result: The <tab name> tab is selected and its panel content is visible in the main content area

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access Course page
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no active session)
steps: 1. In an unauthenticated browser session, navigate to the <Course page URL>
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: the user is redirected to the Login page (visible login form or login prompt); the Course page heading and sections are not visible; Course content is not accessible.

test_cases.test_case: 'Collapse all' fails to collapse expanded sections
preconditions: User is authenticated and has access to the course
Course contains at least two sections
steps: 1. On the Course page, click the chevron for <Section 1> to expand it
2. Click the chevron for <Section 2> to expand it
3. Click the "Collapse all" link at the top right
test_cases.expected_result: The collapse action is blocked: a visible error indicator appears near the top of the page (error banner or toast) stating the collapse failed; all previously expanded sections remain expanded (no section enters the collapsed state); the Course page state is unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: Use 'Collapse all' when many sections are expanded
preconditions: Course Page is open in browser
Course contains multiple sections (>= 5) and each section is individually expanded so activities are visible
steps: 1. Click the 'Collapse all' link
2. Observe the visible state of all section chevrons and activity lists
test_cases.expected_result: All sections visually collapse (chevrons indicate collapsed and activities are hidden); no JavaScript error message or UI artifact appears. Action succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive toggles of a single section's collapse button
preconditions: Course Page is open in browser
At least one section is present and currently expanded
steps: 1. Rapidly click the section's 'Toggle Section Collapse' button repeatedly (multiple consecutive clicks within 1–2 seconds)
2. After clicks stop, observe the final collapsed/expanded state and any UI errors
test_cases.expected_result: The section's visible state stabilizes to reflect the last toggle click (expanded or collapsed as per last click); no persistent UI error or duplicate-state artifact is shown. Action sequence succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Click an activity link then immediately click 'Collapse all' before navigation completes
preconditions: Course Page is open in browser
At least one section is expanded and contains at least one activity
Activity link target may take measurable time to load (simulated slow navigation)
steps: 1. Click an activity's 'Open activity/resource' link
2. Immediately (before the activity page finishes loading) click the 'Collapse all' link
3. Observe whether the browser navigates away, whether collapse takes effect, and whether any error is shown
test_cases.expected_result: Exactly one navigation to the activity occurs and the user does not see a JavaScript error; if navigation completes first the collapse has no visible effect (user is on activity page); if collapse completes first user remains on Course Page with sections collapsed. In all cases the UI does not show an error and the sequence succeeds (no broken state or duplicate actions).

test_cases.test_case: Very long and unusual section/activity names render and remain actionable
preconditions: Course Page is open in browser
Course contains at least one section whose display name is a very long string (>200 characters)
That section contains at least one activity whose display name includes leading/trailing whitespace and non-ASCII characters (emoji / unicode)
steps: 1. Load the Course Page
2. Observe how the long section name is rendered in the section header
3. Hover or focus the long section name (if hover tooltip or accessible label is provided) and observe the full text display
4. Click the long-named activity's 'Open activity/resource' link
test_cases.expected_result: Long section and activity names are rendered without breaking the page layout (e.g., truncated with ellipsis or wrapped in a stable way). Hover or accessibility label exposes the full name (trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace where UI normalizes it). Clicking the activity link successfully opens the activity page and no UI error is shown. Overall interaction succeeds.

modules.module: Course Edit Mode and Activity Chooser

test_cases.test_case: Enable Edit mode shows inline authoring controls and bulk toolbar
steps: 1. Click 'Turn editing on' (Edit_Mode_Toggle) on the Course page

test_cases.test_case: Search activity tiles in Activity Chooser

test_cases.test_case: Select an activity tile and open its creation form (Assignment)

test_cases.test_case: Mark an activity tile as favorite in the Activity Chooser

test_cases.test_case: Activity Chooser Add button enables only after selecting a tile

test_cases.test_case: Add a nested subsection using '+ Add a subsection'

test_cases.test_case: Quick rename a section inline

test_cases.test_case: Quick rename an activity inline

test_cases.test_case: Duplicate a section via the section menu

test_cases.test_case: Hide an activity via the activity menu
steps: 1. Click 'Turn editing on' to enable edit mode

test_cases.test_case: Open 'Set access restrictions' for an activity
steps: 1. Click 'Turn editing on' to enable edit mode

test_cases.expected_result: The Access restrictions panel is displayed for the activity

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open Activity Chooser when Edit mode is OFF

steps: 1. Ensure Edit mode is OFF

test_cases.expected_result: The '+ Add an activity or resource' control is not present / not clickable; the Activity Chooser modal does not open; no inline authoring controls appear in the course page (sections and activities remain unchanged)

test_cases.test_case: Attempt inline quick rename (edit icon) while Edit mode is OFF
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Course page
The course contains sections and/or activities
steps: 1. Ensure Edit mode is OFF
2. Try to locate and click the inline quick rename (edit icon) on a section title

test_cases.expected_result: Inline quick rename edit icons are not visible / not interactive when Edit mode is OFF; clicking where the icon would be does not open an inline edit field; section and activity titles remain unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Add' in Activity Chooser with no tile selected (precondition violation)
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Course page
steps: 1. Enable Edit mode
2. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' to open the Activity Chooser modal
3. Ensure no activity/resource tile is selected in the Activity_Tiles grid
4. Click the Activity Chooser 'Add' button
test_cases.expected_result: The Activity creation form does not open; the Activity Chooser modal remains open; an inline validation message appears on the Activity_Tiles area indicating a tile must be selected (e.g. an inline error on the tiles area or a disabled Add button); no new activity/resource is created

test_cases.test_case: Attempt section-level menu actions (Delete) when Edit mode is OFF
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Course page
Course contains at least one section
steps: 1. Ensure Edit mode is OFF
2. Attempt to open the section-level three-dot menu for a section
3. Attempt to invoke the 'Delete' action from that menu
test_cases.expected_result: The section-level menu is not visible / menu actions are not available when Edit mode is OFF; the 'Delete' action cannot be invoked; the section is not removed or modified

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to select activities for bulk actions when Edit mode is OFF
preconditions: User is signed in and on the Course page
steps: 1. Ensure Edit mode is OFF
2. Attempt to locate and click the activity selection checkbox on an activity row
3. Observe the Bulk Actions toolbar area
test_cases.expected_result: Activity selection checkboxes are not visible / not interactive when Edit mode is OFF; the Bulk Actions toolbar is not visible; no activities can be selected for batch operations

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open an Activity Creation form directly without using Activity Chooser Add (form precondition violation)

2. Observe what is displayed
test_cases.expected_result: The Activity creation form is not presented directly; the UI blocks direct access and either redirects the user to the Course page / Activity Chooser or displays an inline message indicating the activity must be created via the Activity Chooser; no new activity is created

test_cases.test_case: Activity Chooser Add button with no tile selected is blocked
preconditions: User is on the Course page
steps: 1. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' on a section
2. Wait for the Activity Chooser modal to open
3. Ensure no activity/resource tile radio is selected
4. Click the 'Add' button in the Activity Chooser
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking 'Add' is blocked / error shown: the Add action does not open a creation form; the Add button remains disabled or an inline validation message indicating 'a tile must be selected' is shown and the Activity Chooser modal stays open

test_cases.test_case: Selecting a tile then clicking Add opens the creation form (boundary pass for selection precondition)
preconditions: User is on the Course page
steps: 1. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' on a section
2. Wait for the Activity Chooser modal to open
3. Select exactly one activity/resource tile radio (one tile only)
4. Click the 'Add' button in the Activity Chooser
test_cases.expected_result: Opening the selected activity's creation form succeeds: the Activity Chooser closes and the Activity Creation Form for the selected tile is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly toggling Edit mode off while Activity Chooser is open closes the chooser and removes authoring controls
preconditions: User is on the Course page
steps: 1. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' on a section
2. Wait for the Activity Chooser modal to open
3. Immediately click 'Turn editing off' while the modal is still visible
test_cases.expected_result: Action succeeds: Edit mode is turned off, authoring inline controls are removed and the Activity Chooser modal is dismissed; no creation form is opened

test_cases.test_case: Selections and Bulk Actions toolbar cleared when Edit mode is disabled immediately after selecting activities

steps: 1. Click the Activity Selection checkbox for activity A
2. Click the Activity Selection checkbox for activity B
3. Observe the Bulk Actions toolbar becomes visible
4. Click 'Turn editing off' immediately after the toolbar appears
test_cases.expected_result: Disabling edit mode succeeds: the Bulk Actions toolbar is hidden and all activity selection checkboxes are cleared (no activities remain selected); no bulk action is executed

test_cases.test_case: Inline quick rename with a very long name (200+ characters) to probe truncation/validation behavior
preconditions: User is on the Course page
A section is present with an inline quick rename control
steps: 1. Click the section's Inline quick rename edit icon
2. Enter a very long name (200+ characters) into the inline name input
3. Confirm the rename (press Enter or click Save in the inline control)
test_cases.expected_result: Either the rename succeeds or is blocked/error shown: if the system accepts names of this length the rename succeeds and the section header shows the saved name (if truncated by UI it is visually truncated in the header); if the system enforces a maximum name length the action is blocked and an inline validation error indicating the name exceeds the maximum length is shown

test_cases.test_case: Inline quick rename with leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed on save
preconditions: User is on the Course page
A section is present with an inline quick rename control
steps: 1. Click the section's Inline quick rename edit icon

test_cases.expected_result: Rename succeeds and leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed: the saved section title displayed in the UI contains no leading or trailing spaces

test_cases.test_case: Toggling a tile's Favorite star does not satisfy the 'tile selected' precondition — Add remains blocked

steps: 1. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' on a section

test_cases.expected_result: Clicking 'Add' is blocked / error shown: favoriting a tile does not select it; the Add action does not open a creation form and an inline message or disabled Add state indicates a tile must be selected

test_cases.test_case: Double-clicking Add rapidly after selecting a tile should not open duplicate creation forms

steps: 1. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' on a section
2. Wait for the Activity Chooser modal to open
3. Select one activity/resource tile radio
4. Double-click the 'Add' button rapidly (two clicks in quick succession)
test_cases.expected_result: Action is protected: only one Activity Creation Form instance opens for the selected tile (first click succeeds and the second click is ignored or blocked); no duplicate creation forms are displayed

test_cases.test_case: Save and display opens the newly created assignment's page

test_cases.test_case: Cancel discards changes and closes the form

test_cases.test_case: Enabling 'Cut-off date' shows and enables its date/time picker
steps: 1. Open the Activity Chooser and select 'Assignment' to open the Assignment Creation form

test_cases.test_case: Add a restriction using the Restriction Type picker

test_cases.test_case: Enabling Group submissions reveals group-specific controls

test_cases.test_case: Submit form with required Assignment name left blank

test_cases.test_case: Enter non-integer into Max_Number_Uploaded_Files after enabling file submissions

test_cases.test_case: Set Maximum_Attempts below allowed minimum (min 1)

test_cases.test_case: Max_Number_Uploaded_Files accepts the minimum allowed value when File submissions enabled

test_cases.test_case: Maximum_Points blocks a value one unit below the minimum

steps: 1. Enter a valid value in the Assignment_Name field

test_cases.expected_result: Save is blocked; inline validation error shown on Maximum_Points indicating the value must be at least <minimum value> (is blocked / error shown)

test_cases.test_case: Max_Submission_Size blocks zero (must be positive)

test_cases.test_case: Max_Submission_Size accepts a value just above zero (smallest positive)

steps: 1. Enter a valid value in the Assignment_Name field

3. Attempt to upload a file that is one unit larger than the site upload size limit to the 'New picture upload' field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: The upload is blocked; an inline error is shown on the file upload control indicating the file exceeds the site upload size limit and the profile is not saved (is blocked; error shown)

test_cases.test_case: Enter very long rich text in Description (200+ characters)
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Edit Profile page
General section contains 'Description' rich text editor (AST: 'Description' with format 'rich_text')
steps: 1. Click the 'Expand all' link
2. Click into the 'Description' rich text editor
3. Enter a very long text string (200+ characters) into the 'Description' field
4. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: If the system accepts long rich text, the update succeeds and the saved profile's Description displays the full entered text; if the system truncates or limits rich text, the editor displays a clear truncation/limit indicator and the form is blocked with an inline message (either: form submits successfully and saved Description shows the full text (succeeds) OR submission is blocked and an inline message indicates Description exceeds allowed length (is blocked; error shown))

test_cases.test_case: Use Unicode / emoji / special characters in First name and Last name
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Edit Profile page
General section contains 'First name' and 'Last name' fields (both required in AST)
steps: 1. Click the 'Expand all' link
2. Enter a name containing Unicode characters and emoji into the 'First name' field
3. Enter a name containing Unicode characters and emoji into the 'Last name' field
4. Enter a valid value into the required 'Email address' field
5. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: The profile update succeeds and the profile page displays the saved First name and Last name with the Unicode/emoji characters preserved (succeeds), or if the system disallows such characters the form is blocked with an inline validation error next to the name field (is blocked; error shown)

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in First name / Last name / Email is trimmed on save
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Edit Profile page
General section contains 'First name', 'Last name', and 'Email address' fields (AST marks them required)
steps: 1. Click the 'Expand all' link
2. Enter a value with leading and trailing spaces into the 'First name' field
3. Enter a value with leading and trailing spaces into the 'Last name' field
4. Enter an email value with leading and trailing spaces into the 'Email address' field
5. Click the 'Update profile' button
test_cases.expected_result: The update succeeds and leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed in the saved data; the profile page displays First name, Last name, and Email without the extra spaces (succeeds). If trimming is not performed, the system shows the saved values preserving whitespace or displays an inline normalization warning (visible outcome shown)

test_cases.test_case: Immediate browser Back after successful Update profile to check for duplicate submissions
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Edit Profile page
General required fields can be filled to achieve a successful save (AST: 'Update profile' refreshes the profile page on success)
steps: 1. Fill required fields: enter valid 'First name', 'Last name', and 'Email address'
2. Click the 'Update profile' button and wait for the profile page to refresh
3. Immediately press the browser Back button
test_cases.expected_result: Pressing Back does not trigger a duplicate profile update; only the single intended update exists and the profile page continues to display the saved changes (succeeds). Any attempt by the browser to re-submit the previous POST is prevented by the UI/navigation and no additional changes or duplicate artifacts (e.g. repeated picture uploads) appear (succeeds)

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Log out' redirects to Login page showing login form
preconditions: User is on a protected page where the 'Log out' button is visible
steps: 1. Click the 'Log out' button
test_cases.expected_result: The application redirects to the Login page and the Login form is visible (fields for <username> and <password> and a 'Sign in' button). This matches the on_success behavior: terminates the current authenticated session and redirects to the login page.

test_cases.test_case: After logging out, attempting to open a protected page redirects to Login
preconditions: User is on a protected page where the 'Log out' button is visible
steps: 1. Click the 'Log out' button
2. Enter <protected page URL> in the browser address bar and navigate to it
test_cases.expected_result: Navigating to the protected page redirects to the Login page and the Login form is displayed, demonstrating that access to protected pages requires re-authentication.

test_cases.test_case: 'Log out' button is no longer present after logout and sign-in entry is shown
preconditions: User is on a protected page where the 'Log out' button is visible
steps: 1. Click the 'Log out' button
2. Observe the top navigation/header area
test_cases.expected_result: The Login page is displayed and the header no longer shows the 'Log out' button; a sign-in entry point (e.g., a 'Sign in' link or the Login form) is visible instead.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access the Log out action (precondition not met)
steps: 1. Open the application home page as an unauthenticated user
2. Inspect the page header / navigation area for the Log out control
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Log out' button/control is not visible or is disabled. The UI does not present any clickable logout action; the unauthenticated user cannot initiate logout (no session termination occurs and no redirect to the login page is triggered).

test_cases.test_case: After logging out, accessing a protected page is blocked and requires re-authentication
preconditions: user is authenticated at start of test
steps: 1. Authenticate as <valid user>
2. Click the 'Log out' button
3. After the app redirects to the login page, enter <protected page URL> in the browser address bar and navigate to it
test_cases.expected_result: After step 2 the session is terminated and the app shows the login page. After step 3 the browser is redirected to the login page (protected content is not displayed); the protected page remains inaccessible and the user must re-authenticate to view it.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click on Log out button
preconditions: User is viewing any protected page that shows the 'Log out' button
steps: 1. Ensure the authenticated protected page tab is focused
2. Click the 'Log out' button
3. Immediately (within 1 second) click the 'Log out' button again
test_cases.expected_result: First click succeeds: session is terminated and the user is redirected to the login page. Second click is blocked / error shown: no additional navigation occurs and no duplicate-session error is displayed to the user (the UI remains on the login page).

test_cases.test_case: Logout in one tab invalidates session in another tab (refresh after logout)
preconditions: User has the application open in Tab A and Tab B, both authenticated and on protected pages
steps: 1. In Tab A, focus the tab showing a protected page
2. In Tab A, click the 'Log out' button
3. In Tab B, focus the tab showing a protected page
4. In Tab B, click the browser refresh button (or press Enter in the address bar for the protected page)
test_cases.expected_result: The logout action in Tab A succeeds: Tab A session is terminated and Tab A is redirected to the login page. The refresh/navigation in Tab B is blocked / error shown: protected content is not displayed and Tab B is redirected to the login page (access to protected pages requires re-authentication).

test_cases.test_case: Use browser Back after logout
preconditions: User is on a protected page that navigated from another protected page (browser history exists)
steps: 1. Click the 'Log out' button on the protected page
2. On the resulting login page, press the browser Back button once
3. If the browser Back displays a protected page UI, attempt to interact with a protected action on that page (click a protected link or button)
test_cases.expected_result: Logout succeeds: session is terminated and the user is on the login page. Using the browser Back is blocked / error shown: either the login page remains shown or any returned protected page immediately redirects to the login page when interaction is attempted; protected functionality cannot be used without re-authentication.

test_cases.test_case: Navigate directly to a protected URL after logout (address-bar paste)
preconditions: Tester knows the URL of a protected page
steps: 1. While authenticated, go to any protected page
2. Click the 'Log out' button
3. On the login page, paste the known protected page URL into the browser address bar and press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: Logout succeeds: session is terminated and the user is on the login page. Direct navigation to the protected URL is blocked / error shown: the browser shows the login page (or an immediate redirect to login occurs) and access to the protected page is denied until the user re-authenticates.
